Board memo: INSIDE INFORMATION – Project LAMPLIGHT – Embargo until 14 Aug 2026 07:00 BST; circulation limited to Insiders under UK MAR.
Issuer: Northroyd Bank plc (UK Main Market), Company Secretary: Ms. Priyanka Meldrum; contact: incident@northroyd.example | Tel +44 0 2 0 7 123 0000.
On 12 Aug 2026 we detected a cyber incident at our cloud vendor Brentmill Data Services Ltd (fictional), which temporarily exposed a backup share used by Retail Ops.
Preliminary scoping indicates up to 8,400 customers’ names, emails, and masked account references (e.g., ACCT‑***4921) plus branch sort codes may have been accessed; passwords, full PANs, and NINOs are not held in this share.
Two employee records (contractor rota) include mobile numbers and home postcodes (e.g., ZZ1 1ZZ) for Mr. Owen Talgarth and Ms. Lila Brays, both fictional.
Operational impact is low; systems restored within 47 minutes; ICO notification threshold is under review by DPO and Legal.
In parallel, Project LAMPLIGHT (proposed acquisition of Hearthmere Payments Ltd) reached agreed terms on a definitive Share Purchase Agreement (SPA) for 100% of the equity at £128.5m cash, plus a 20% earn‑out contingent on FY27 EBITDA.
The draft SPA includes a customary MAE clause; based on current analysis we do not propose to assert a Material Adverse Effect, and the Target’s rep states “no Material Adverse Change since 30 June 2026”.
This incident and the SPA pricing assumptions constitute Inside Information; the issuer’s securities are placed on the restricted list and market soundings are suspended for non‑wall‑crossed investors.
Investor‑relations draft line for the H1 update is prepared but must not be disseminated before the embargo: “We have executed a definitive agreement to acquire Hearthmere Payments Ltd; completion expected in Q4, subject to FCA change of control consents”.
For the avoidance of doubt, widely reported 2024 NCSC guidance on third‑party storage risks is public and generic, and this reference does not itself contain inside information.
pm note: chase brentmill root‑cause report, update risk register, schedule post‑incident wash‑up; spa‑day vouchers for branch staff are postponed and not market‑sensitive.
Case Ref: NB/SEC/2026‑08/0O1l1; ticket INC‑NB‑I1O‑8A; S 3    bucket tag: “northroyd‑arch1ve‑bk‑2026” (note obfuscated identifiers).
Vendor link: https://portal.brentmill.example/notice?cid=nbplc&event=lamplight-14-08-26 and internal log: https://sec.northroyd.example/kibana/app/discover#/?_q=INC‑NB‑I1O‑8A.
Prepared by Mr. Alistair Perwick (Chief Risk Officer) and approved by Ms. Priyanka Meldrum (DPO); queries to inside‑list‑coordinator@northroyd.example; not for release via a Primary Information Provider before 14 Aug 2026.
